name: Alto de Penouta – circular desde Tapia de Casariego
region_intro: Road cycling around Tapia De Casariego offers diverse landscapes, from rugged coastal cliffs to rolling green hills and historic villages. The region, part of Spain's "Green Spain," features a mix of challenging terrain with significant elevation gains and more moderate coastal paths. Road cyclists can expect varied routes that traverse both the Cantabrian Sea coastline and inland areas. The area provides a scenic backdrop for cycling adventures, with many routes on paved surfaces suitable for road bikes. Best road cycling routes around Tapia De Casariego The most popular road cycling route is Lago Galicia y Ribadeo – circular desde Tapia de Casariego, a 24.8 miles (39.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views towards Galicia and Ribadeo. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto de Penouta – circular desde Tapia de Casariego, a difficult 59.6 miles (95.9 km) path. This route includes a significant climb to Alto de Penouta, providing a challenging experience. Local road cyclists also love the Alto de Penouta – Navia loop from Tapia de Casariego, a 51.3 miles (82.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with substantial elevation gain, often completed in about 4 hours 34 minutes. Road cycling around Tapia De Casariego is defined by rugged cliffs, rolling green hills, and charming coastal villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal rides to challenging inland ascents. The routes in Tapia De Casariego are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.94 stars from over 28 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Tapia De Casariego's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling routes can I find around Tapia de Casariego?
A: Tapia de Casariego offers a diverse range of road cycling routes. You'll find scenic coastal rides with breathtaking views of the Cantabrian Sea, as well as more challenging inland routes that feature significant climbs through rolling green hills. The region caters to various fitness levels, from moderate coastal paths to demanding ascents.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Tapia de Casariego?
A: Yes, the region is known for its challenging terrain, including climbs that have been part of Spain's Vuelta bike race. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070399364/alto-de-penouta-circular-desde-tapia-de-casariego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Penouta – circular desde Tapia de Casariego&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 95.9 km with over 1,700 meters of elevation gain, offer a significant test for very good fitness levels. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20161542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Bobia Ridge – Folgueiras loop from Tapia de Casariego&lt;/a&gt;, covering 82.7 km with over 1,500 meters of ascent.

Q: What are some scenic coastal road cycling routes in the area?
A: For stunning coastal vistas, consider routes that hug the Cantabrian Sea. The region's 'Senda Costera' offers extensive coastline to explore. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077411122/lago-galicia-y-ribadeo-circular-desde-tapia-de-casariego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago Galicia y Ribadeo – circular desde Tapia de Casariego&lt;/a&gt;, a 39.9 km route that provides beautiful views towards Galicia and Ribadeo.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while road cycling near Tapia de Casariego?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle past dramatic cliffs and numerous beaches such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porcía Beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/359293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penarronda Beach&lt;/a&gt;, which is also a Natural Monument. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/852558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de la Cruz&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5561255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de la Atalaya&lt;/a&gt; viewpoints offer spectacular ocean vistas along the coast.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or charming villages to visit on a road cycling trip?
A: Absolutely. Tapia de Casariego itself is a picturesque fishing village with a strong maritime heritage. You can explore its port and the historic San Sebastián neighborhood. Nearby, you might encounter the charming town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/359284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadeo&lt;/a&gt;, or historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/366948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Cabo Blanco&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient fort.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Tapia de Casariego?
A: The region, part of 'Green Spain,' generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling. While specific weather can vary, the spring and autumn months often provide a good balance of mild temperatures and less rainfall, making them ideal for exploring both coastal and inland routes. Summers are also popular, though can be warmer.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Tapia de Casariego?
A: There are 14 road cycling routes available around Tapia de Casariego, catering to various skill levels. This includes 2 easy routes, 5 moderate routes, and 7 difficult routes, ensuring options for every road cyclist.

Q: What do other road cyclists think about the routes in Tapia de Casariego?
A: The road cycling routes in Tapia de Casariego are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.94 out of 5 stars from over 28 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from rugged coastal cliffs to rolling green hills, and the variety of routes available for different fitness levels.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes that extend beyond Tapia de Casariego?
A: Yes, Tapia de Casariego serves as an excellent starting point for longer excursions. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38184966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ramón de Campoamor Park – Puente de los Santos loop from Tapia de Casariego&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 116 km, can take you into neighboring areas and showcase varied terrain, including the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/368090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de los Santos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Tapia de Casariego?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in Tapia de Casariego. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077411122/lago-galicia-y-ribadeo-circular-desde-tapia-de-casariego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago Galicia y Ribadeo – circular desde Tapia de Casariego&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070399364/alto-de-penouta-circular-desde-tapia-de-casariego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Penouta – circular desde Tapia de Casariego&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for your ride.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on road cycling routes in Tapia de Casariego?
A: You can expect a mix of terrain. Coastal routes often feature well-paved surfaces with gentle undulations, while inland routes can include significant climbs and descents through rolling green hills. The region is characterized by its rugged cliffs and diverse landscapes, offering a varied and engaging cycling experience, mostly on paved roads suitable for road bikes.
tips: Tapia, capital of the council of Tapia de Casariego, stands out for its strong ties to fishing and the canning industry. Walk through its beautiful fishing and sports port.
Beautiful section for road bike lovers near Tapia de Casariego.

El Alto de Penouta is another classic from western Asturias. We can choose between several variants, on its north and west faces to climb up to an altitude of 780 meters above sea level. From the top we can see the coast and the distant town of Tapia de Casariego and/or the spectacular valley of the Navia river.

waypoints.name: Rural Road Near Tapia de Casariego
